questions.q: Which hero of the Ramayana is honoured with the title 'Maryada Purushottam'?
questions.explanation: Rama is called Maryada Purushottam, the ideal man who never transgressed the bounds of virtue.

questions.q: What does the title 'Maryada Purushottam' essentially mean?
options: The most perfect or supreme man within proper bounds
questions.explanation: 'Maryada Purushottam' means the supreme man who always stayed within the bounds of virtue, duty, and honour.

questions.q: The word 'maryada' in the title chiefly refers to what?
questions.explanation: 'Maryada' means proper limits or bounds of conduct, which Rama never crossed.

questions.q: In which kingdom was Rama, the Maryada Purushottam, born as a prince?
questions.explanation: Rama was born as prince of Ayodhya, capital of the Kosala kingdom.

questions.q: Who was the father of Rama?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha of Ayodhya was the father of Rama.

questions.q: Embodying his ideal, Rama accepted how many years of exile to honour his father's word?
questions.explanation: Rama accepted fourteen years of forest exile to uphold his father's promise.

questions.q: Which sage composed the original Ramayana that celebrates Rama as the ideal man?
questions.explanation: The sage Valmiki composed the original Sanskrit Ramayana praising Rama's perfect virtue.

questions.q: Who was the devoted wife of Rama?
questions.explanation: Sita, daughter of King Janaka, was the wife of Rama.

questions.q: Which moral principle is most associated with Rama as Maryada Purushottam?
questions.explanation: Rama is the supreme upholder of dharma, righteous duty, throughout the Ramayana.

questions.q: Rama is traditionally regarded as an avatar of which deity?
questions.explanation: Rama is revered as the seventh avatar of the god Vishnu.

questions.q: Which queen demanded the boons that sent Rama into exile, testing his perfect obedience?
questions.explanation: Queen Kaikeyi demanded Rama's exile and Bharata's coronation, which Rama accepted without protest.

questions.q: When Bharata begged him to return and rule, what did Rama insist on doing as the ideal son?
options: Returning at once to take the throne
Completing the full term of exile first
questions.explanation: Rama refused the throne and insisted on completing his vowed exile to honour his father's word.

questions.q: Which brother insisted on accompanying Rama into exile to serve him?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana left Ayodhya to accompany and serve Rama throughout the fourteen-year exile.

questions.q: What did Bharata place upon the throne of Ayodhya while ruling in Rama's absence?
questions.explanation: Bharata enthroned Rama's wooden sandals and ruled only as his regent until Rama's return.

questions.q: Who was the king of the vanaras (monkeys) whom Rama helped, illustrating his sense of just alliance?
questions.explanation: Rama allied with Sugriva, helping him regain the throne of Kishkindha in exchange for aid in finding Sita.

questions.q: Which rakshasa king abducted Sita, prompting Rama's righteous war?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the demon king of Lanka, abducted Sita, leading to Rama's war against him.

questions.q: Rama is sometimes called 'Ekavachani', meaning he upheld which principle?
options: He spoke only one (true) word and kept it
He ruled for only one year
questions.explanation: Rama is praised as a man of one word, always true to his promise, reflecting his perfect honour.

questions.q: Tradition also calls Rama 'Ekapatni-vrata', meaning he was devoted to what?
options: A vow to a single wife
questions.explanation: Rama observed 'Ekapatni-vrata', remaining faithful to his one wife Sita, unusual for kings of his time.

questions.q: Which devoted monkey hero first found Sita in Lanka, aiding Rama's righteous mission?
questions.explanation: Hanuman leapt across the ocean and located Sita in Ravana's Ashoka grove in Lanka.

questions.q: Which of Ravana's brothers, choosing righteousness over kinship, joined Rama's side?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana abandoned the unrighteous Ravana and joined Rama, who later crowned him king of Lanka.

questions.q: The ideal of conduct that Rama never transgressed is best summarized by which pairing?
questions.explanation: As Maryada Purushottam, Rama never transgressed the bounds of virtue, duty, and honour.

questions.q: Rama belonged to which illustrious royal dynasty, lending weight to his role as the model king?
questions.explanation: Rama was born into the Ikshvaku or Solar (Suryavamsha) dynasty, a line celebrated for righteous kings.

questions.q: In how many books (kandas) is Valmiki's Ramayana, which portrays Rama's ideal life, traditionally divided?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana is traditionally divided into seven kandas, from the Bala Kanda to the Uttara Kanda.

questions.q: Rama's ideal of just rule gave rise to which famous expression for a perfect reign?
questions.explanation: Rama's righteous and prosperous reign is idealized as 'Rama Rajya', the model of perfect governance.

questions.q: When Rama killed the vanara king Vali, what later distinguishes this act in discussions of his maryada?
options: He struck from concealment, which is debated as a moral test of dharma
He broke a personal vow of non-violence
questions.explanation: Rama slew Vali with an arrow from hiding, an act long discussed in relation to the subtleties of dharma.

questions.q: To uphold a king's duty toward his subjects' doubts, what did Rama ultimately do regarding Sita after returning to Ayodhya?
options: He banished her to the forest despite her innocence
He abandoned the throne with her
questions.explanation: Heeding public doubt about Sita's purity, Rama sent her to the forest, a tragic act done in the name of royal duty.

questions.q: Who composed the verses of the Ramayana that Lava and Kusha, Rama's sons, later sang in his court?
questions.explanation: Lava and Kusha, raised in Valmiki's hermitage, sang the Ramayana that Valmiki himself had composed.

questions.q: In the Uttara Kanda, what proof of Sita's purity occurs at the end of her life, vindicating her virtue?
options: The Earth opens and receives her
She ascends a chariot to heaven
She is restored to the throne
questions.explanation: Sita calls on Mother Earth, who opens and takes her back, affirming her purity in the Uttara Kanda.

questions.q: The popular retelling 'Ramcharitmanas', which deeply spread Rama's ideal, was written in Awadhi by which poet?
questions.explanation: Tulsidas composed the Ramcharitmanas in Awadhi, popularizing Rama as the supreme ideal of conduct.

questions.q: As the model of kingly duty, the figure of Rama is invoked in which Indian ethical and political ideal still cited today?
options: Good governance modeled on Rama Rajya
A philosophy of renunciation of all rule
questions.explanation: Rama as Maryada Purushottam underpins the enduring ideal of Rama Rajya, a benchmark for just and good governance.
